name: Chrysanthemum Longevity
description: The Chrysanthemum, particularly the yellow variety, holds a revered position in Chinese mythology and culture, deeply intertwined with themes of longevity, rejuvenation, and nobility. It is considered one of the 'Four Gentlemen' of flowers, alongside the plum blossom, orchid, and bamboo, representing noble virtues and refined taste. The Chrysanthemum Longevity is believed to possess potent life-extending properties, often associated with Taoist immortals who consumed it to achieve immortality. Its association with autumn, a time of decline and harvest, paradoxically symbolizes resilience and enduring beauty in the face of adversity. In imperial China, the chrysanthemum was the emblem of the 9th month and was often depicted on imperial robes and artifacts, signifying nobility and longevity. Consuming chrysanthemum tea or wine, especially on the Double Ninth Festival (Chongyang Festival), is a traditional practice intended to ward off misfortune and promote health. The flower is also used in traditional Chinese medicine for its purported health benefits, including detoxification, cooling the body, and improving eyesight. Its vibrant colors and intricate petal arrangements are seen as manifestations of cosmic harmony and the cyclical nature of life. Across various dynasties, the Chrysanthemum Longevity has been a source of inspiration for poets, painters, and artisans, who have captured its essence in countless works of art, solidifying its place as a symbol of enduring beauty and the pursuit of a long and prosperous life. Its presence in gardens and homes is believed to bring good fortune and protect against negative energies. The flower is also presented as a gift to elders as a wish for longevity and health.
subtitle: Symbol of Longevity and Nobility

keyMyths.title: Taoist Immortals and Chrysanthemum
keyMyths.description: Taoist immortals were believed to consume Chrysanthemum to achieve immortality. This belief solidified the flower's association with long life and spiritual transcendence.

sources.author: Li Shizhen
sources.description: Comprehensive encyclopedia of traditional Chinese medicine, detailing the medicinal properties of chrysanthemum.
